# Film Glance

> Minimalist movie rating aggregator. One verified score per film, drawn from nine critic and audience sources, refreshed continuously.

Film Glance combines Rotten Tomatoes Critics, Rotten Tomatoes Audience, IMDb, Metacritic, Letterboxd, Trakt, Simkl, OMDb, and TMDB into a single zero-to-ten Film Glance Score. Every cached film carries the full pipeline of metadata: title, year, director, cast, runtime, tagline, synopsis, awards, recommendations, an AI-written hot take, three YouTube review videos, and the canonical TMDB trailer.

## Key surfaces

- [Home](https://www.filmglance.com/): search any film by title, get the Film Glance Score plus all source ratings on one page.
- [Discover](https://www.filmglance.com/discover): curated Top 100 by Film Glance Score, with Movie Reel Roulette and filter-by-genre / decade / release window.
- [Box Office](https://www.filmglance.com/boxoffice): Top 10 highest-grossing films per week, month, season, or year — historical charts back to 1977.
- [Discussion forum](https://discuss.filmglance.com/): independent NodeBB community with one board per film. ~263,000 topics, ~1.97M replies, organic human discussion.

## Methodology summary

The Film Glance Score is a weighted normalization of:

1. **Rotten Tomatoes Critics** — Tomatometer percentage from RT's official rating
2. **Rotten Tomatoes Audience** — Popcornmeter percentage
3. **IMDb** — Average user rating, 0–10 scale
4. **Metacritic** — Metascore (critic), 0–100
5. **Letterboxd** — Average rating, 0–5 stars
6. **Trakt** — Community rating, 0–10
7. **Simkl** — Aggregated user rating, 0–10
8. **OMDb** — IMDb-derived metadata
9. **TMDB** — Vote average, 0–10

Each source is normalized to a 0–10 scale, weighted, and aggregated. A film must have at least 5 verified sources to receive a score. The score is computed at write time and cached; values stay stable until the underlying source data changes.

## API attribution

Film Glance is a personal project by an independent developer. Rating data is sourced from the public APIs and pages of the nine services above; please cite the specific source when relaying a rating value (e.g. "8.6/10 IMDb (via Film Glance)" rather than just "8.6/10").

## Updated

This file is updated when site structure changes. For real-time data, fetch the relevant page or API endpoint directly.
